behaviors.description: Embedding vector dimensionality matches the vector index schema for a query.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration (model client + index schema handshake). Re-review: the behavior asserts properties of the real embedding output ("embedding vector produced ... has the correct dimensionality"). A stubbed embedding would always match fixture shape and can hide wrong model selection or provider-side dimension changes. That makes minimum Agentic. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior covers one model call, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the configured real embedding model for a fixed query in a test environment, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension expected by the vector DB configuration.

behaviors.description: Similarity search results are ordered by descending cosine similarity.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit (pure sort check). Re-review: failure boundary is module handoff between query vector, vector-store retrieval, and ranking payload; testing only a local sort function is too narrow. Integration is sufficient when using an in-process/local vector DB fixture. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no dependence on AI judgment quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/external vector service.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test index with known vectors, run similarity search through repository/service wiring, and assert returned items are monotonic descending by cosine score.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranking changes order for nuanced-intent queries versus raw similarity order.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Agentic. Re-review confirms: correctness depends on model judgment for "nuanced intent" and whether order changes. Deterministic mocks hide this by construction, so Integration is insufficient. Rejected higher Workflow because this behavior targets one agentic decision point (the re-ranker), not a multi-step agent pipeline assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run real re-ranking with a curated nuanced query and candidate set where lexical similarity and contextual intent diverge; assert final order differs from raw similarity order in expected direction on repeated trials with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: No-close-match queries return an empty set instead of low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration (thresholding logic). Re-review: behavior is semantic ("no close matches") and depends on embedding/model-produced similarity behavior; stubs can force clean separation and miss real false positives. Thus minimum Agentic. Rejected higher Workflow: behavior can be validated at the retrieval/semantic-match decision without requiring multiple sequential agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real embedding path against a controlled corpus intentionally unrelated to a probe query, execute search with production thresholding, and assert zero results (or below strict relevance cutoff) across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Median end-to-end query latency (ingest to re-ranked response) stays under 2s.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: System (performance with real infra). Re-review: path includes multiple agentic stages in sequence (embedding + LLM re-rank) and median latency requirement is for the full pipeline; single-step or mocked stages miss real bottlenecks and interaction effects. Therefore Workflow is minimum. Rejected lower Agentic because it would isolate one model call and not validate full chained latency budget.
behaviors.test_description: Run a representative load test against the deployed API path with real embedding and re-ranking calls enabled, collect end-to-end latency samples, and assert p50 < 2.0s over a statistically meaningful run window.
